urlparse.__returns:
Returns 4 strings, representing the scheme, the hostname, the path, and the parameters fields of the URL.
If any component is not found, returns a nil value instead of a string.
If the string cannot be parsed, returns false.
urlparse.__example:
local net = require "net"
local console = require "console"

local url = console.readln('Enter a URL : ')
local scheme, hostname, path, parameters = net.urlparse(url)

scheme = scheme or error("The URL entered is invalid")

print("scheme :", scheme)
print("hostname :", hostname)
print("path\t:", path)
print("parameters :", parameters)

__params.__description: A string representing a URL to parse, for example "https://www.luart.org/index.html".

urlparse.__description: Parse a URL into 4 components, corresponding to the structure of a URL : scheme://hostname /path?parameters

ip.__example:
local net = require "net"

local function GetLocalIP ()
	return net.ip
end    

print(GetLocalIP()) -- Public and local ip addresses are different. Should output "192.168.x.x", or "127.0.0.x" (if offline.)

__description: The net module provides networking functionalities
__tinydesc: Supports networking operations including sockets, FTP, and HTTP requests

select.__returns:
• In case of success, returns true.
  • In case of error, returns false (you can check error message with the net.error property).
  • If no networking event occured and the time limit expired, returns nil
select.__example:
-- Multi-connection Server example
local net = require "net"

-- create the server listening Socket
local server = net.Socket("127.0.0.1", 5000)

-- array of clients Sockets
local sockets = {server}
server.disconnected = false

if server:bind() then
  print("Server running on 127.0.0.1:5000")
else
  error("Network error : cannot create the server Socket")
end

while true do
  if net.select(sockets) == false then
    error("Network error : "..net.error)
    break
  end
  
  local alive_sockets = {}
  for client in each(sockets) do
    -- check for readability 
    if client.canread then
      if client == server then
        if server.canread then
          -- check for new connection
          local newclient = server:accept()
          print(newclient.ip.." has connected")
          sockets[#sockets+1] = newclient
        end
      else
        local data = client:recv()
        if data == false then
          print(client.ip.." has disconnected")
          client.disconnected = true
        else
          print(client.ip..": "..tostring(data))
        end
      end			
    end
    -- check for error
    if client.failed then
      if client == server then
        error("fatal network error with server")
      else
        print(client.ip.." has encountered a fatal network error")
        client.disconnected = true
      end
    end
    if not client.disconnected then
      alive_sockets[#alive_sockets+1] = client
    end
  end
  -- keep only connected clients
  sockets = alive_sockets
end

__params.__description: A table containing an array of Sockets to check for network events.
An optional number to indicate the maximum time for select to wait for network events, in microseconds. If that argument is not provided, the function wait until a network event occurs.

select.__description: Check for network events (readability, writability and errors) on the provided array of Sockets, by updating their corresponding properties Socket.canread, Socket.canwrite, Socket.failed.
select.__tinydesc: Check for network events on Sockets

port.__description: The Ftp.port property gets the current port used for connection to the FTP server or nil if connection have been closed.
port.__example:
local net = require "net"

local url = "your ftp server address"
local ftpclient = net.Ftp(url, "anonymous", "guest")

-- outputs ftp port
print("ftp port = "..ftpclient.port)

command.__returns: This method return a Task instance. Once the Task has finished, it will return a boolean value indicating if the operation was successful.
command.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- connect to the FTP server
local url = "your ftp server"
local client = net.Ftp(url, "username", "password")

-- Execute the LIST command
if await(client:command("LIST /")) then
  print("LIST command result :")
else
  error(net.error)
end

__params.__description: A string that contains the command to execute.
An optional boolean value indicating if the Task should wait for a response.

command.__description: Sends and executes the provided command to the FTP server.

movefile.__returns: This method return a Task instance. Once the Task has finished, it will return a boolean value indicating if the operation was successful.
movefile.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- connect to the FTP server
local url = "your ftp server"
local client = net.Ftp(url, "username", "password")

if await(client:movefile("test.txt", "/archive/test.txt")) then
  print("Successfully moved FTP directory 'test.txt' to '/archive/test.txt'")
else
  error(net.error)
end

__params.__description: A string that contains the file path (partially or fully qualified file names relative to the current FTP directory can be used).
A string that contains the new file path (partially or fully qualified file names relative to the current FTP directory can be used).

movefile.__description: Moves the specified file from the FTP server.
movefile.__tinydesc: Move a file on the server

__params.__description: A string representing a valid hostname to connect to the FTP server. The string can contain a port, a username and password (in this case, the following parameters are not needed), with the followinf format :
An optional string representing the username (use "anonymous" for anonymous access if supported by the server).
An optional string representing the password (use any string for anonymous access).

constructor.__description: The Ftp constructor returns a Ftp value representing a FTP client and connects it to the FTP server at the given URL, using provided authentication.
constructor.__example:
local net = require "net"

local url = "ftp://your ftp server address"
local client = net.Ftp(url, "anonymous", "guest")
print("Sucessfully connected to '"..url.."'")

proxy.__returns: This function returns a boolean value indicating if the operation was successful.
proxy.__example:
local net = require "net"

local client = net.Ftp("ftp://yourftpserver.org")

-- set a proxy connection with the server at 88.32.132.44 using port 8080
client:proxy("88.32.132.44:8080")

__params.__description: An optional string representing the proxy server address.
An optional string representing the proxy user name.
An optional string representing the proxy password.

proxy.__description: Sets the FTP proxy server connection.

upload.__returns:
Returns true if uploading to the FTP server was successful, or false otherwise.
This method return a Task instance. Once the Task has finished, it will return a boolean value indicating if the operation was successful.
upload.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- connect to the FTP server
local url = "your ftp server"
local client = net.Ftp(url, "username", "password")

-- upload file "test.txt"
if await(client:upload(File("test.txt"))) then
  print("Successfully uploaded 'test.txt' to  "..url)
else
  error(net.error)
end

__params.__description:
An optional string that contains the destination path and filename on the server.
If not provided, the file will be uploaded on the current FTP directory (See Ftp.currentdir) using the same filename.

upload.__description: Upload a file to the connected FTP server.
upload.__tinydesc: Upload a file on the server

Ftp.__description: The Ftp object connects to FTP servers to upload, download, and manage files remotely

list.__returns:
Returns two values at each iteration :
  • A string that contains the element name
  • A boolean value set to true if the current element is a directory, or false if it's a file.
list.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- connect to official ftp Ubuntu server
local client = net.Ftp("ftp://ftp.ubuntu.com", "anonymous", "guest")
print("Directory /")

-- Iterate over each directory element
for name, isdir in client:list("*.*") do
	if isdir then
		print("\t"..name)
	else
		print("\t/"..name)
	end
end

__params.__description:
An optional string that contains a valid directory path or file name. The string can contain wildcards, but no blank spaces are allowed.
If not specified, iterate over all current directory contents.

list.__description: Iterates over directory content inside the specified search path.

hostname.__description: The Ftp.hostname property gets the current hostname string used for connection to the FTP server or nil if connection have been closed.
hostname.__example:
local net = require "net"

local url = "your ftp server address"
local ftpclient = net.Ftp(url, "anonymous", "guest")

-- outputs ftp hostname
print("ftp hostname = "..ftpclient.hostname)

close.__example:
local net = require "net"

local url = "your ftp server address"
local ftpclient = net.Ftp(url, "anonymous", "guest")

-- closes the connection
client:close()
close.__description:
Close the Ftp client connection.
Remember to close the connection when no more needed. Ftp:close() is automatically called before garbage collection of the FTP object, but this behaviour cannot be predicted

makedir.__returns: This method return a Task instance. Once the Task has finished, it will return a boolean value indicating if the operation was successful.
makedir.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- connect to the FTP server
local url = "your ftp server"
local client = net.Ftp(url, "username", "password")

-- make directory "/test/"
if await(client:makedir("/test/")) then
  print("Successfully created FTP directory '/test/'")
else
  error(net.error)
end

__params.__description: A string that contains the name of the directory to be created. This can be either a fully qualified path or a name relative to the current FTP directory.

makedir.__description: Creates a new directory on the FTP server.
makedir.__tinydesc: Create a directory on the server

removefile.__returns: This method return a Task instance. Once the Task has finished, it will return a boolean value indicating if the operation was successful.
removefile.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- connect to the FTP server
local url = "your ftp server"
local client = net.Ftp(url, "username", "password")

-- remove file "/test.txt"
if await(client:removefile("/test.txt")) then
  print("Successfully removed FTP file '/test.txt'")
else
  error(net.error)
end

__params.__description: A string that contains the name of the directory to be removed. This can be either a fully qualified path or a name relative to the current FTP directory.

removefile.__description: Removes the specified file from the FTP server.
removefile.__tinydesc: Remove a file on the server

download.__returns:
This method return a Task instance. Once the Task has finished, it will return one of the following possible values :
  • File value representing the downloaded file. If the file already exists in the current directory, it will be overwritten.
  • false value if the operation failed. You can get more information with the net.error property.
download.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- connect to official ftp Ubuntu server
local client = net.Ftp("ftp://ftp.ubuntu.com", "anonymous", "guest")

-- download file "favicon.ico" to the current directory
if await(client:download("/releases/favicon.ico")) then
    print("Successfully downloaded 'favicon.ico' from ftp://ftp.ubuntu.com")
else
    print(client.log)
end

__params.__description: A string that contains the name of the file to be retrieved from the FTP server.
An optional string that contains the name of the file in the current local directory. If not provided, the remote filename will be used.

download.__description: Download a remote file from the FTP server, to the current local directory.
download.__tinydesc: Download a file from the server

removedir.__returns: This method return a Task instance. Once the Task has finished, it will return a boolean value indicating if the operation was successful.
removedir.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- connect to the FTP server
local url = "your ftp server"
local client = net.Ftp(url, "username", "password")

-- remove directory "/test/"
if await(client:removedir("/test/")) then
  print("Successfully removed FTP directory '/test/'")
else
  error(net.error)
end

__params.__description: A string that contains the name of the directory to be removed. This can be either a fully qualified path or a name relative to the current FTP directory.

removedir.__description: Removes the specified directory from the FTP server.

currentdir.__description:
The Ftp.currentdir property gets or sets the current directory on the FTP server as a string.
Returns nil if the value could not be retrieved (get more information with the net.error property).
currentdir.__example:
local net = require "net"

local url = "your ftp server address"
local ftpclient = net.Ftp(url, "anonymous", "guest")

-- outputs ftp port
print("ftp port = "..ftpclient.port)
currentdir.__tinydesc: Get/set the current directory on the server

getmime.__returns: Return a string representing the fully qualified MIME type, or nil if the MIME type cannot be found.
getmime.__example:
local console = require "console"
local net = require "net"

local mime = net.getmime(console.readln('Enter a filename : '))

print('MIME type : '..mime)

__params.__description:
The value to retrieve the MIME type from, can be any of the following values :

  • A File value.
  • A string representing an URI or a filename.

getmime.__description: Returns the fully qualified domain from an IP address (reverse DNS lookup).

error.__description: The net.error property gets the last network error message or nil
error.__example:
local net = require "net"

print("net.error = "..(net.error or "none"))

-- error : calling net.select() with an empty array
net.select({})

print("net.error : "..net.error)
error.__tinydesc: Get the last network related error message

isalive.__description: The net.isalive property checks if a network connection is available, returning true or false otherwise.
isalive.__example:
if require("net").isalive then
	print("This computer is connected to the network")
else
	print("This computer is not connected to the network")
end
isalive.__tinydesc: Check if a network connection is available

port.__description: The Socket.port property gets the port number used to create the Socket.
port.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- create and connect a client TCP socket to the host "google.com"
local socket = net.Socket("google.com", 80)

-- outputs 80
print("port = "..socket.port)

blocking.__description:
The Socket.blocking property sets or checks if the Socket is in blocking mode.
  • When set to true, all Socket operations (such as reading and sending data) will block until they have terminated.
  • When set to false, all Socket operations will return immediately a Task object that will perform asynchronously.
By default, all created Socket objects have the property Socket.blocking set to true
blocking.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- create and connect a client TCP socket to the host "google.com"
local socket = net.Socket("google.com", 80)

-- sets the Socket in blocking mode (the default)
socket.blocking = true
socket:connect()

-- socket:send() will block until all data has been sent
socket:send("GET / HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: www.google.fr\r\nConnection: Close\r\n\r\n")

-- wait until some data is available
local data = socket:recv()

-- prints received data (an http response)
print(data)

starttls.__returns:
Returns true if TLS has been successfully enabled.
In case of error, returns false. Error message can be obtained with the net.error property.
starttls.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- create a socket to www.google.com on port 80
local socket = net.Socket("www.google.com", 80)

if socket:connect() then
	print("Connected to www.google.com !")
	local sent = socket:send("GET / HTTP/1.1\r\nHost:www.google.com\r\n\r\n")
	print("Bytes sent : "..sent)
end

__params.__description:
An optional string representing the certificate to verify the identity of the other peer. If none is provided, uses the certificate from the server. The string can be :

  • A server name of a certificate already installed in the current user/Windows certificate store
  • A path to a .pfx file (PKCS #12), that contains cryptographic information (certificates and private keys). Its contents can be cryptographically protected with passwords.
An optional string representing the password that must be provided when using a .pfx file .

starttls.__description: Enable transport level security (TLS -formerly known as SSL) on a Socket connection. The connection must have been initialized with a previous Socket:bind() or Socket:connect() call. Any further calls to Socket:send() or Socket:recv() will be encrypted/decrypted, respectively.

accept.__returns:
Returns one of the following possible values :
  • Socket, usable to send or recv data from the new connection.
  • false if an error occured.
  • Task object to perform the operation asynchronously if the Socket is non-blocking, returning one of the previous values once finished.
accept.__example:
-- Echo Server example
local net = require "net"

-- create the server listening Socket
local server = net.Socket("127.0.0.1", 5000)

-- not needed, socket.blocking is already set to true (default)
server.blocking = true
server:listen()

local client = server:accept()
print('Connected with ', client.ip)

-- echo to the client the data just received
while true do
	local data = client:recv()
	if data == false then
		break
	end
	client:send(data)
end
accept.__results: Socket | boolean | Task
accept.__description: Accept a connection. The socket should have been set to listen for incoming connections with a previous Socket:bind() call.

ip.__description: The Socket.ip property gets the IP address the socket is bound to as a string
ip.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- create and connect a client TCP socket to the host "google.com"
local socket = net.Socket("google.com", 80)

-- outputs socket.ip
print("socket.ip = "..socket.ip)

Socket.__description: The Socket object sends and receives data over network connections

nodelay.__description:
The Socket.nodelay property get/set the TCP_NODELAY option on the socket, to control Nagle algorithm.
  • When set to false Nagle algorithm is enabled, TCP will wait to send small amounts of data until the acknowledgment for the previous data is received.
  • When set to true Nagle algorithm is disabled (the default), TCP will send small amounts of data even before the acknowledgment for previous data sent is received.

Example
nodelay.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- create and connect a client TCP socket to the host "google.com"
local socket = net.Socket("google.com", 80)

-- outputs false (the default Socket.nodelay value)
print("TCP_NODELAY = "..tostring(socket.nodelay))

peek.__returns: Returns the number of bytes available for reading.
peek.__example:
-- Echo Server example
local net = require "net"

-- create the server listening Socket
local server = net.Socket("127.0.0.1", 5000)

-- enable server Socket to receive incomming connections
server:listen()

local client = server:accept()

-- echo to the client the data just received
while true do
	local nbytes = client:peek()
	local data = client:recv(nbytes)
	if data == false then
		break
	end
	client:send(data)
end
peek.__description: Get how many bytes are available for reading on the Socket connection with a single Socket:recv() call.

connect.__returns:
• true if the operation succedded, or false if an error occured.
  • Task object to perform the operation asynchronously if the Socket is non-blocking, returning one of the previous values once finished.
connect.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- create a socket to www.google.com on port 80
local socket = net.Socket("www.google.com", 80)

if socket:connect() then
	print("Connected to www.google.com !")
	socket:send("GET / HTTP/1.1\r\nHost:www.google.com\r\n\r\n")
	-- wait to be sure to receive all the header data
	sleep(300)
	
	local nbytes = socket:peek()
	print("Length of header: "..nbytes)
	print("Length of data received : "..#socket:recv())
end
connect.__results: boolean | Task
connect.__description: Connect to the remote Socket.ip address. Used in blocking mode, the methods wait until the connection is established, or returns immediately otherwise.

close.__example:
-- Echo Server example
local net = require "net"

-- create the server listening Socket
local server = net.Socket("127.0.0.1", 5000)

-- not needed, socket.blocking is already set to true (default)
server.blocking = true
server:listen()

local client = server:accept()
print('Connected with ', client.ip)

-- echo to the client the data just received
while true do
	local data = client:recv()
	if data == false then
-- client:close() not needed, will be called upon client garbage collection	
		break
	end
	client:send(data)
end
close.__description:
Closes the Socket and the bounded connection. The Socket should not be used anymore.
When the Socket is garbage collected, the close method is called automatically

canwrite.__description: The Socket.canwrite property gets the writability of a Socket after a net.select() call.
canwrite.__tinydesc: Check if Socket is writable after a net.select() call

send.__returns:
Returns one of the following possible values :
  • true if the operation succedded, or false if an error occured.
  • Task object to perform the operation asynchronously if the Socket is non-blocking, returning one of the previous values once finished.
send.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- create a socket to www.google.com on port 80
local socket = net.Socket("www.google.com", 80)

if socket:connect() then
	print("Connected to www.google.com !")
	local sent = socket:send("GET / HTTP/1.1\r\nHost:www.google.com\r\n\r\n")
	print("Bytes sent : "..sent)
end
send.__results: boolean | Task | nil

__params.__description: A Buffer or a string containing the data to be sent. If another type of variable is used, it is internaly converted to a string before sending.

send.__description: Send all the data to the Socket. In blocking mode, the method can block if the length of the data is larger than the maximum allowed by the transport.

bind.__returns: Returns a boolean value indicating if the operation is successful.
bind.__example:
-- Echo Server example
local net = require "net"

-- create the server listening Socket
local server = net.Socket("127.0.0.1", 5000)

-- enable server Socket to receive incomming connections
server:bind()

local client = server:accept()

-- echo to the client the data just received
while true do
	local data = client:recv()
	if data == false then
		break
	end
	client:send(data)
end

__params.__description: An optional integer representing the maximum length of the queue of pending connections (defaults to 5).

bind.__description: Bind the Socket and listen for incoming connection.
bind.__tinydesc: Bind and listen for incoming connections

family.__description: The Socket.family property gets the address family "ipv4" or "ipv6"
family.__example:
local net = require "net"

local socket = net.Socket("google.com", 80)

-- outputs "ipv4"
print("socket.family = "..socket.family)

failed.__description:
The Socket.failed property checks the Socket for errors after net.select() call.
Returns true if an error occured during last net.select() call, or false otherwise.
failed.__tinydesc: Check if Socket is in error state after a net.select() call

shutdown.__returns: The function returns no value.
shutdown.__description: Shuts down the Socket. Further sends or reveives are disallowed, and any pending data is discarded. Call this method if you want to stop using the connection immediately, because Socket:close() can take some time to effectively close the connection.

__params.__description: A string representing an IP address (family is autodetected) or a hostname (IP is autoresolved to the given family)
The port number the Socket will be bound to.

An optional string to specify the address family when resolving the provided hostname.

  • "ipv4" : an IPv4 address will be used when resolving the hostname (default)
  • "ipv6" : an IPv6 address will be used when resolving the hostname

constructor.__description: The Socket constructor returns a Socket value representing a Windows TCP socket to be bound to a specific IP address or hostname on the given port number. In case of hostname, a DNS query is sent to be resolved to a valid IP of the given family.
constructor.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- valid 
local sock = net.Socket("www.google.com", 80)

-- valid 
sock = net.Socket("smtp.google.com", 80)

-- error : 'http:\www.google.com' is an URL not a hostname
sock = net.Socket("http:\\www.google.com", 80)

recv.__returns:
Returns one of the following possible values :
  • Buffer containing the received bytes.
  • false if an error occured.
  • Task object to perform the operation asynchronously if the Socket is non-blocking, returning one of the previous values once finished.
If the connection has been lost, Socket:recv() returns false and the net.error property returns nil.
recv.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- create a socket to www.google.com on port 80
local socket = net.Socket("www.google.com", 80)

if socket:connect() then
	print("Connected to www.google.com !")
	socket:send("GET / HTTP/1.1\r\nHost:www.google.com\r\n\r\n")
	-- wait to be sure to receive all the data
	sleep(300)
	print("Data received : "..socket:recv())
end
recv.__results: Buffer | boolean | Task | nil

__params.__description: An optional integer representing the maximum number of bytes to read from the connection, defaults to 1024.

recv.__description: Receive data from the Socket. In blocking mode, the method wait until some data is available for reading.

canread.__description: The Socket.canread gets the readability of a Socket after a net.select() call.
canread.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- create the server listening Socket
local server = net.Socket("127.0.0.1", 5000)

-- array of clients Sockets
local sockets = {server}

if server:bind() and server:listen() then
	print("Server running on 127.0.0.1:5000")
	while true do
		if net.select(sockets) == false then
			error("Network error : "..net.error)
			break
		end
		-- if a new client connects, send the "Hello World !" message and close the Socket
		if server.canread then
			local client = server:accept()
			client:send("Hello World !")
			client:close()
		end
	end
end
canread.__tinydesc: Check if Socket is readable after a net.select() call

publicip.__description: The net.publicip property gets the current public IPv4 address as a string. A valid Internet connection must be available, or returns false otherwise.
publicip.__example:
local net = require "net"

print("Public IPv4 IP : "..net.publicip)
publicip.__tinydesc: Get the current public IPv4 address

resolve.__returns:
• In case of success, returns the IP address associated with the DNS record, represented by a string.
  • If no DNS record was found, returns nil
  • In case of error, returns false (you can check error message with the net.error property).
resolve.__example:
local console = require "console"
local net = require "net"

console.write('Enter hostname : ')
local ip = net.resolve(console.readln()) or "not found";

print('IP address associated with that hostname : '..ip)

__params.__description: The string to indicate the hostname to resolve, for example "luart.org".

An optional string to indicate the IP address family to retrieve from the DNS record :

  • "ipv4" : an IPv4 address will be returned (default)
  • "ipv6" : an IPv6 address will be returned

resolve.__description: Resolves the given hostname to an IP address from the DNS record.

useragent.__description: The Http.useragent property gets or sets the User-Agent header string sent with requests.
useragent.__example:
local net = require "net"
local client = net.Http("https://api.example.com")
client.useragent = "MyLuaRTApp/1.0"

timeout.__description: The Http.timeout property gets or sets the request timeout in milliseconds. Set to 0 to disable timeout (the default).
timeout.__example: client.timeout = 5000 -- 5 seconds timeout to get a response from the server

verifyssl.__description: The Http.verifyssl property gets or sets the SSL certificate verification. Set to false to ignore invalid or self-signed certificates.
verifyssl.__example:
local net = require "net"
local client = net.Http("https://self-signed.local")
client.verifyssl = false

redirect.__description: The Http.redirect property enables or disables automatic following of HTTP 3xx redirects.

protocol.__description: The Http.protocol property gets or sets the HTTP version (1 for HTTP/1.1, 2 for HTTP/2).

interface.__description: The Http.interface property gets or sets the network interface (IP address as a string) used for the connection.
interface.__example: client.interface = "192.168.1.100" -- use this specific network interface

port.__description: The Http.port property gets the port number used for connection to the HTTP server or nil if connection have been closed.
port.__example:
local net = require "net"

local httpclient = net.Http("https://www.luart.org")

-- outputs 443
print("http port = "..httpclient.port)

delete.__returns:
This method return a Task instance. Once the Task has finished, it will return two values :
  • The first value is the Http instance used for the PUT request.
  • The second value is nil in case of error or a table that contains the server response, with the following fields
    • "content" : a string containing the body part of the HTTP response
    • "elapsed" : a number representing the elapsed time to get the response
    • "status" : a number containing the HTTP response status
    • "reason" : a string containing HTTP response status text
    • "ok" : a boolean value indicating if the GET request succeeded
    • "headers" : a table containing a pair of strings(header field names of the HTTP response as key and content as values)
    • "cookies" : a table containing a pair of strings(cookies names of the HTTP response as key and content as values)
delete.__example:
local net = require "net"

local client = net.Http("https://httpbun.com")

-- make a DELETE request, and once its terminated, call the provided function, to process the response
client:delete("/delete", data).after = function (self, response)
  if not response then
    error(net.error)
  end
  print(response.content)
end

waitall()

__params.__description: A stringrepresenting the target resource on the server to delete.
A File or any value converted to a string representing the body content of the request.

delete.__description: Send an asynchronous DELETE request to the connected HTTP server to delete the specified resource on the server.
delete.__tinydesc: Send an HTTP DELETE request to the server

hostname.__description: The Http.hostname property gets the current hostname string used for connection to the HTTP server or nil if connection have been closed.
hostname.__example:
local net = require "net"

local httpclient = net.Http("https://www.luart.org")

-- outputs "www.luart.org"
print("http hostname = "..httpclient.hostname)

__params.__description: A string representing a valid URL to connect to the HTTP server.
An optional string representing a username for basic HTTP authentication.
An optional string representing a password for basic HTTP authentication.

constructor.__description: The Http constructor returns a Http value representing a HTTP client and connect it to the HTTP server at the given URL, using optional basic authentication.
constructor.__example:
local net = require "net"

local url = "https://www.luart.org"
local client = net.Http(url)
print("Sucessfully connected to '"..url.."'")

content.__description: The Http.content property gets a string, representing the current content of the response server from the last HTTP request.
content.__example:
local net = require "net"

local client = net.Http("http://download.thinkbroadband.com")
local task = client:download("/10MB.zip")

local last = 0

while not task.terminated do
    sleep()
    local size = #client.content
    if size > 0 then
        print(((size-last) or size).." new bytes received")
        last = size
    end
end
content.__tinydesc: Get the current received response from the server

setratelimit.__example: client:setratelimit(1024 * 1024) -- set maximum download rate to 1MB/s

__params.__description: The maximum download speed in bytes per second.
An optional maximum upload speed in bytes per second.

setratelimit.__description: Sets the maximum bandwidth rate for data transfer

__params.__description: A string representing the bearer authentication token.

setbearer.__description: Sets the Bearer Authentication token for the session (OAuth2).

proxy.__returns: This function returns a boolean value indicating if the operation was successful.
proxy.__example:
local net = require "net"

local client = net.Http("https://www.google.com")

-- set a proxy connection with the server at 88.32.132.44 using port 8080
client:proxy("88.32.132.44:8080")

__params.__description: An optional string representing the proxy server address.
An optional string representing the proxy user name.
An optional string representing the proxy password.

patch.__returns:
This method return a Task instance. Once the Task has finished, it will return two values :
  • The first value is the Http instance used for the PUT request.
  • The second value is nil in case of error or a table that contains the server response, with the following fields
    • "content" : a string containing the body part of the HTTP response
    • "elapsed" : a number representing the elapsed time to get the response
    • "status" : a number containing the HTTP response status
    • "reason" : a string containing HTTP response status text
    • "ok" : a boolean value indicating if the GET request succeeded
    • "headers" : a table containing a pair of strings(header field names of the HTTP response as key and content as values)
    • "cookies" : a table containing a pair of strings(cookies names of the HTTP response as key and content as values)
patch.__example:
local net = require "net"

local client = net.Http("https://reqbin.com")

local data = [[
{
  "User": {
    "Id": 12345,
    "Name": "John Smith"
  }
}
]]

-- make a PATCH request, and once its terminated, call the provided function, to process the response
client:patch("/echo/patch/json", data).after = function (self, response)
  if not response then
    error(net.error)
  end
  print(response.content)
end

waitall()

__params.__description: A stringrepresenting the target resource on the server.
A File or any value converted to a string representing the body content of the request.

patch.__description: Send an asynchronous PATCH request to the connected HTTP server to apply partial modifications to the target resource.
patch.__tinydesc: Send an HTTP PATCH request to the server

close.__example:
local net = require "net"

local url = "https://www.luart.org"
local client = net.Http(url)
print("Connected to "..url)

-- closes the connection
client:close()

get.__returns:
This method return a Task instance. Once the Task has finished, it will return two values :
  • The first value is the Http instance used for the GET request.
  • The second value is nil in case of error or a table that contains the server response, with the following fields
    • "content" : a string containing the body part of the HTTP response
    • "elapsed" : a number representing the elapsed time to get the response
    • "status" : a number containing the HTTP response status
    • "reason" : a string containing HTTP response status text
    • "ok" : a boolean value indicating if the GET request succeeded
    • "headers" : a table containing a pair of strings(header field names of the HTTP response as key and content as values)
    • "cookies" : a table containing a pair of strings(cookies names of the HTTP response as key and content as values)
get.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- creates a Http instance and call its get() method
-- the get() method will return a Task instance
-- then we set the Task instance "after" property
-- with a function that will be called once the Task is terminated, using the two values returned by the Task

net.Http("https://wttr.in"):get("/Paris?format=3").after = function (client, response)
  console.write(response.content)
end

waitall()

__params.__description: A string representing the resource to get on the server.
An optional table that contains parameters to be sent with the request, in the form of key1=value1&key2=value2...

get.__description: Send an asynchronous GET request to the connected HTTP server for a specific resource.
get.__tinydesc: Send an HTTP GET request to the server

response.__description: The Http.response property returns a table that contains the HTTP response of the last request.
response.__example:
local net = require "net"

local client = net.Http("http://download.thinkbroadband.com")
await(client:download("/10MB.zip"))

if client.response then
  print("Successfully downloaded "..client.response.file.name)
end
response.__tinydesc: Get the response table from the last HTTP request

headers.__description: The Http.headers property returns a table that contains the headers of the requests to be sent to the server (where keys and values are strings representing header names and values respectively).
headers.__example:
local net = require "net"

local client = net.Http("https://httpbin.org")

-- add header "Content-Type" with the value "text/plain" for next requests
client.headers["Content-Type"] = "text/plain"

-- the data to be posted (in text/plain format)
local data = [[Hello World !]]

-- make a POST request, and after its terminated, call the provided function, to process the response
client:post("/post", data).after = function (self, response)
  if not response then
    error(net.error)
  end
  print(response.content)
end

waitall()
headers.__tinydesc: Get the headers table for the next HTTP request

Http.__description: The Http object sends requests and receives responses from web servers

post.__returns:
This method return a Task instance. Once the Task has finished, it will return two values :
  • The first value is the Http instance used for the POST request.
  • The second value is nil in case of error or a table that contains the server response, with the following fields
    • "content" : a string containing the body part of the HTTP response
    • "elapsed" : a number representing the elapsed time to get the response
    • "status" : a number containing the HTTP response status
    • "reason" : a string containing HTTP response status text
    • "ok" : a boolean value indicating if the POST request succeeded
    • "headers" : a table containing a pair of strings(header field names of the HTTP response as key and content as values)
    • "cookies" : a table containing a pair of strings(cookies names of the HTTP response as key and content as values)
post.__example:
local net = require "net"

local client = net.Http("https://reqbin.com")

local data = [[{
  "Id": 12345,
  "Customer": "John Smith",
  "Quantity": 1,
  "Price": 10.00
}]]

-- make a POST request, and after its terminated, call the provided function, to process the response
client:post("/echo/post/json", data).after = function (self, response)
  if not response then
    error(net.error)
  end
  print(response.content)
end

waitall()

__params.__description: A stringrepresenting the resource on the server to POST parameters to.

An optional table (for multipart/form-data content) or a string (for raw data content) that contains parameters to be sent with the request.Each key of the provided table must be a string (representing the parameter name) and each value represents the value for the corresponding parameter (a string or a File object).

post.__description:
Send an asynchronous POST request to the connected HTTP server.
If the parameters table contains a File object, the request is sent as multipart/form-data. Otherwise, it is sent as application/x-www-form-urlencoded.
post.__tinydesc: Send an HTTP POST request to the server

download.__returns:
This method return a Task instance. Once the Task has finished, it will return two values :
  • The first value is the Http instance used for the PUT request.
  • The second value is nil in case of error or a table that contains the server response, with the following fields
    • "file" : a File object instance representing the downloaded file. The file is dowloaded in the current directory.
    • "elapsed" : a number representing the elapsed time to get the response
    • "status" : a number containing the HTTP response status
    • "reason" : a string containing HTTP response status text
    • "ok" : a boolean value indicating if the GET request succeeded
    • "headers" : a table containing a pair of strings(header field names of the HTTP response as key and content as values)
    • "cookies" : a table containing a pair of strings(cookies names of the HTTP response as key and content as values)
download.__example:
local net = require "net"

-- Download a server resource and waits until it's done
local client, response = await(net.Http("http://download.thinkbroadband.com"):download("/10MB.zip"))
print("Downloaded "..response.file.name..", "..response.file.size.." bytes")

__params.__description: A string representing the target resource on the server to be downloaded.

download.__description: Send an asynchronous GET request to the connected HTTP server to download the specified resource from the server.
download.__tinydesc: Download a file from the server

received.__description: The Http.received property gets a number, representing the byte count of data received from the server during last HTTP request.
received.__example:
local net = require "net"

local client = net.Http("http://download.thinkbroadband.com")
local task = client:download("/10MB.zip")

local last = 0

while not task.terminated do
    sleep()
    last = client.received-last
    if last > 0 then
        print("Received "..last.." bytes")
    end
end
received.__tinydesc: Get the byte count of data received from the server

cookies.__description: The Http.cookies property returns a table that contains the cookies of the requests to be sent to the server (where keys and values are strings representing cookies names and values respectively).
cookies.__example:
local net = require "net"

local client = net.Http("https://httpbun.org")

-- Add a cookie "name" associated with the next request URI for the host "https://httpbun.org", with the value "LuaRT"
client.cookies = { name = "LuaRT" }

-- make a GET request, and after its terminated, call the provided function, to process the response
client:get("/cookies").after = function (self, response)
  if not response then
    error(net.error)
  end
  -- Should print the Cookie value
  print(response.content)
end

waitall()
cookies.__tinydesc: Get or set the cookies table for the next HTTP request

put.__returns:
This method return a Task instance. Once the Task has finished, it will return two values :
  • The first value is the Http instance used for the PUT request.
  • The second value is nil in case of error or a table that contains the server response, with the following fields
    • "content" : a string containing the body part of the HTTP response
    • "elapsed" : a number representing the elapsed time to get the response
    • "status" : a number containing the HTTP response status
    • "reason" : a string containing HTTP response status text
    • "ok" : a boolean value indicating if the GET request succeeded
    • "headers" : a table containing a pair of strings(header field names of the HTTP response as key and content as values)
    • "cookies" : a table containing a pair of strings(cookies names of the HTTP response as key and content as values)
put.__example:
local net = require "net"

local client = net.Http("https://httpbun.com")

local data ="Hello World !"

-- make a PUT request, and once its terminated, call the provided function, to process the response
client:put("/put", data).after = function (self, response)
  if not response then
    error(net.error)
  end
  print(response.content)
end

waitall()

__params.__description: A stringrepresenting the target resource on the server.
A File or any value converted to a string representing the body content of the request.

put.__description: Send an asynchronous PUT request to the connected HTTP server to creates a new resource or replaces a representation of the target resource.
put.__tinydesc: Send an HTTP PUT request to the server

adapters.__returns:
Returns an iterator function to be used in a for loop, to iterate over network adapters, returning two values at each iteration :
  • A string, representing the name of the adapter.
  • A table, that contains the list of all the IP associated with the adapter.
adapters.__example:
local net = require "net"

print("Available Network adapters :")
for name, ip in net.adapters("ipv4") do
	print(ip[1].."\t"..name)
end

__params.__description: A string representing the IP family address returned at each iteration : "ipv4" or "ipv6".

adapters.__description: Iterator function to retrieve installed network adapters on the Windows operating system, using the IPv4 or IPv6 address family.

reverse.__returns: Return a string representing the fully qualified hostname.
reverse.__example:
local console = require "console"
local net = require "net"

console.write('Enter IP adress : ')
local host = net.reverse(console.readln()) or "not found";

print('hostname : '..host)

__params.__description: The IPv4 or IPv6 address to perform the reverse DNS lookup.

reverse.__description: Returns the fully qualified domain from an IP address (reverse DNS lookup).
reverse.__tinydesc: Get the fully qualified domain from an IP address

urlencode.__example:
local net = require "net"

local s = "Hello world!"
print("raw:    ", s)
print("encoded:", net.urlencode(s))

__params.__description: The string to URL-encode.

urlencode.__description: Returns an URL-encoded string, so that it can be safely transmitted over the internet
